name: Mega Auto Finance
description_short: Mega Auto Finance purchases retail installment contracts from California auto dealerships, specializing in non-prime and sub-prime automotive financing with real-time manual underwriting.
description_long:
Mega Auto Finance was founded in April 2012 and is headquartered in San Diego, California. The company operates as an acceptance company that purchases retail installment sale contracts from franchised and independent automotive dealerships throughout California. The founding insight was recognizing a gap in the market: consumers underserved by traditional banking systems who struggled to finance vehicles through conventional lenders.

Mega Auto Finance provides automotive financing solutions exclusively through its dealer network. The company does not lend directly to consumers; instead, it purchases installment contracts that dealers originate. They offer financing programs specifically designed for the non-prime and sub-prime credit spectrum, targeting customers who may not qualify for traditional bank financing. The company emphasizes real-time manual underwriting of every application submitted by dealers in their network, aiming to find affordable programs tailored to individual applicant circumstances.

The company distinguishes itself through several operational commitments: manual underwriting rather than automated decisioning, real-time application review, strict due diligence including direct verification of consumer information, and emphasis on ethical business practices. Their stated values include honesty, excellence, passion, innovation, confidence, and respect. They position themselves as a trusted partner to both their dealer network and customers throughout the car buying and financing experience.

Mega Auto Finance serves a legitimate market function in providing credit access to subprime auto buyers through dealer partnerships. However, as a subprime lender, customers should expect higher interest rates and stricter contract terms typical of this lending category. The company's focus on California dealerships and B2B relationships (with dealers rather than direct consumer lending) means consumers cannot apply directly. Prospective borrowers must work through participating dealerships.
pros: Real-time manual underwriting of every application rather than automated decisioning
Direct verification of consumer information as part of underwriting process
Founded in 2012 with 12+ years of operational history in the market
Specializes in non-prime and sub-prime financing, explicitly serving underbanked populations
Network of franchised and independent dealerships across California
Emphasis on finding affordable programs tailored to specific budgets and needs
Ethical business practices and long-term dealer partnerships as stated priorities
cons: Subprime lender, so customers will face significantly higher interest rates than prime borrowers
Only operates in California—not available to consumers in other states
No direct consumer application process; borrowers must apply through participating dealerships only
Limited public information available about specific APRs, terms, or loan products
Purchases contracts from dealers rather than originating loans, limiting transparency about final terms
best_for: California residents with non-prime or subprime credit seeking auto financing through dealership partnerships
Auto dealerships in California seeking financing programs to expand their customer base in the subprime market
Consumers unable to qualify for traditional bank auto loans who need transportation financing
diagnosis: Mega Auto Finance is best for California auto dealerships seeking to expand financing options for subprime customers, and for consumers with non-prime credit who need vehicle financing through participating dealerships. The main caveat is that this is a B2B model (working with dealers) rather than direct consumer lending, and customers should expect subprime interest rates and terms commensurate with their credit profile.
services: Retail installment contract purchasing from automotive dealerships
Real-time manual underwriting of auto financing applications
Dealer financing programs to support vehicle sales
Consumer customer servicing on purchased contracts
Direct verification of consumer information during underwriting
Credit decision making tailored to individual applicant circumstances
